content: AnthropicClient accepts optional _client parameter for dependency injection in tests. Avoids prototype-level spying on Anthropic.messages which does not exist on the prototype (instance property). Pattern: inject dependencies for testability without modifying the public API.

name: cc-client-testability
description: CC subprocess backend exports pure functions (serializeMessages, buildPrompt, parseCcOutput) for direct unit testing without spawning processes. Integration tests use a StubCcClient subclass that overrides call() to return controlled output.

name: anthropic-sdk-0.39-breaking-types
description: SDK 0.39.0: TextBlock.citations required (use null), Usage.cache_* fields required number|null (not optional). Check CHANGELOG on upgrades.

name: integration-canonical-types
description: Canonical src/types.ts defines all shared types (Tool, ToolRegistry, ContextManager, BudgetUtilization, etc.); sub-module types.ts files re-export from ../types.ts rather than defining locally. Prevents type mismatches across parallel-built modules.

content: ToolRegistry class uses toDefinitions() not definitions(). The ToolRegistry interface in src/types.ts defines register/get/list/toDefinitions. Test files and loop.ts must call toDefinitions().

name: parallel-mvp-build
description: Sapling MVP built via 5 parallel leads in ~55 min: scaffold, tools (50 tests), client (20 tests), context manager (67 tests), agent loop (13 tests). Integration lead reconciled cross-module types. 164 total tests, ~2300 lines source.
ov merge fails when working tree has unstaged changes to files modified by the merge branch
resolution: Commit or stash unstaged changes before running ov merge. Check git status for clean working tree.

description: CcClient pipe deadlock: awaiting proc.exited before draining stdout/stderr causes permanent hang when claude subprocess fills the OS pipe buffer (~64KB).
resolution: Use Promise.all([proc.exited, stdout.text(), stderr.text()]) to drain concurrently. Also set stdin:'ignore' to prevent claude waiting for input.

name: claude-cli-subprocess-flags
description: Claude Code CLI flags for subprocess invocation: use '--system-prompt <content>' (NOT --system-prompt-file), '--json-schema <json-string>' (inline JSON, NOT a file path), '--tools ""' disables all tools, '--output-format json' returns {type,result,usage} envelope. Strip CLAUDECODE env var from subprocess env to avoid nested sessions error.
CcClient --max-turns 1 with --json-schema causes error_max_turns (num_turns:2) because JSON schema validation/retry counts as an extra turn internally.
resolution: Remove --max-turns when using --tools '' since disabled tools already prevent agentic loops. The model responds once and exits.

name: cc-plain-text-fallback
description: When --json-schema is combined with --tools '', claude ignores the schema and returns plain text in the result field. CcClient must handle this gracefully: if JSON.parse(result) fails, treat result as text_response with no tool_calls. This makes the loop terminate naturally (no tool calls = task complete).

content: The agent loop (src/loop.ts) must print the final text response to stdout when the task completes. Extract text blocks from the last assistant response and write them with process.stdout.write(). Without this, the model's answer is silently swallowed.

Logger configure() and setColorEnabled() must be called in src/index.ts after loadConfig() to wire --verbose/--quiet/--json CLI flags to the logging subsystem. configure() sets level/json/quiet; setColorEnabled(false) is called when quiet=true.

description: CcClient parsed raw.result (plain text summary) instead of raw.structured_output (structured JSON with tool_calls). claude CLI puts schema output in structured_output, not result. This caused all tool calls to be missed — loop saw 0 tool_use blocks and reported task_complete after 1 turn.
resolution: Add structured_output field to CcRawResponse interface; prefer raw.structured_output over raw.result in parse logic; fall back to result for legacy compat.

content: CcClient tool injection: when request.tools.length > 0, append '## Available Tools' section to systemPrompt listing each tool's name, description, and input_schema as JSON. Ensures LLM uses exact Sapling tool names (bash, read, write, edit, grep, glob) rather than guessing CC built-in names.

The claude CLI has no --max-turns flag. CcClient uses --output-format json, --json-schema, --tools '' to get single-turn structured output. Turn limiting is unnecessary because --tools '' prevents the subprocess from executing its own tools/looping.

name: reshape-consecutive-user-fix
description: reshapeMessages inserts a synthetic assistant [Acknowledged] message between task(user) and archive(user) to prevent consecutive user messages that violate the Anthropic API alternating-role requirement. isArchiveMessage() also filters this ack so it does not accumulate in history across turns.
ctx-pipeline pruning no-op: scoreMessages(historyMessages, files, 0) caused all history msgs to get 'task' or 'current' category (never 'history')
resolution: Pass historyMessages.length as currentTurnIdx; guard categorizeMessage index===0→'task' with currentTurnStart < totalMessages

content: CC session auto-detect: loadConfig() checks CLAUDECODE env var; if set and SAPLING_BACKEND not set, defaults backend to 'sdk'. Explicit SAPLING_BACKEND=cc wins. Prevents nested CC subprocess sessions when running inside Pi/CC.

name: sdk-error-classification
description: SDK error classification: classifySdkError(err) maps HTTP status codes to typed codes: 401->SDK_AUTH_FAILED, 403->SDK_PERMISSION_DENIED, 404->SDK_MODEL_NOT_FOUND, 429->SDK_RATE_LIMITED, 529->SDK_OVERLOADED. Status extracted via 'status' in err narrowing (no any). SDK_AUTH_FAILED, SDK_PERMISSION_DENIED, SDK_MODEL_NOT_FOUND, SDK_NOT_INSTALLED added to UNRECOVERABLE_CODES in loop.ts.

name: archive-tool-result-narrowing
description: findToolResult in archive.ts must use unknown narrowing to detect ToolResultBlock in user messages. ContentBlock union excludes tool_result to avoid breaking other consumers; instead export ToolResultBlock separately from types.ts and use isToolResultBlock(rawBlock: unknown) guard with const rawBlock: unknown = block inside the for-of loop.
Adding tool_result to ContentBlock union breaks cc.ts and measure.ts which access .name and .input without narrowing.
resolution: Export ToolResultBlock as a separate type from types.ts, not part of ContentBlock. Use unknown narrowing in archive.ts to safely access tool_result blocks at runtime.

name: json-console-wiring
description: Wire --json through config: add json field to SaplingConfig/RunOptions, default false in DEFAULT_CONFIG, pass through loadConfig() spreads. In action handler: call configure({verbose,quiet,json}) + setColorEnabled(!quiet && !json) after loadConfig(). Use printJson/printJsonError for result envelope; use logger.info() for human-readable summary so quiet/verbose/json modes work consistently.

name: ctx-fileHash-staleness
description: detectFileModifications() calls Bun.hash(content) on write/edit inputs and stores in archive.fileHashes; detectHashStaleFiles() fingerprints read results and marks paths stale when content differs between reads — merged into modifiedFiles before pruning to catch bash-modified files not tracked via write/edit

content: ctx-scoring-weights-v2: score.ts weights (sum=1.0): recency 0.30, file-overlap 0.25, error 0.20, decision 0.12, unresolved-question 0.08, size-penalty 0.05. The unresolved-question signal (assistant messages ending with '?') was previously tracked in metadata but unused in score calculation — now wired up.

name: ctx-grep-pruning
description: prune.ts pruneUserMessageContent() routes text blocks through looksLikeGrepOutput() heuristic (>50% of lines match file:line: pattern) before bash truncation; large grep outputs get summarized via summarizeGrepResult() to a compact file-list format. summarizeUserToolResult() compresses old low-score block-form user messages into one-liners.

name: bench-harness-design
description: Synthetic benchmark harness for context pipeline uses pre-built Message[] traces (no real LLM calls). User messages use string content (not tool_result blocks) to avoid estimateBlockTokens crash on non-ContentBlock types. Baseline vs managed comparison measures total input tokens across all turns; LONG (100-turn) scenario achieves ≥30% reduction.
estimateBlockTokens in context/measure.ts only handles ContentBlock types (text, tool_use). Passing tool_result blocks (NOT in ContentBlock union) causes crash on .name/.text access.
resolution: Use string content for user messages in synthetic tests, not tool_result block arrays. Or add type guard in estimateBlockTokens for tool_result.

name: parallel-bugfix-batch
description: 14 issues across 4 leads in ~28 min. Decomposition: cc-client-lead (3 bugs, src/client/cc.ts), context-lead (3 bugs + tuning + bench, src/context/), cli-logger-lead (4 logger/CLI tasks, src/index.ts + src/logging/), sdk-tools-lead (SDK robustness + grep fix, src/client/anthropic.ts + src/tools/grep.ts). Non-overlapping file areas. Context lead phased internally: bugs first (3 parallel builders), then tuning + bench (2 parallel builders). All merges clean except one auto-resolve on src/index.ts.

name: unblocked-issues-post-batch
description: Fixing 14 issues unblocked 4 downstream: sapling-ff53 (Overstory Phase 1, blocked by 237f), sapling-48ad (CC tool name mismatch, blocked by 4e5d), sapling-a01f (CC test mock stale format, blocked by 501a), sapling-a58d (pruneMessages index misalignment, blocked by 7c7d).

content: cc-tool-name-normalization: CcClient normalizes structured.tool_calls[].name to lowercase before building tool_use ContentBlocks. Prevents 'Tool not found' errors when CC LLM returns capitalized names (Write, Read, Bash) vs Sapling's lowercase ToolRegistry keys. System prompt also includes explicit directive: 'MUST use exact tool names', 'Do not use capitalized names'.

description: pruneMessages index misalignment: after filtering dropped messages, kept[i] no longer corresponds to scoredMessages[i].
resolution: Create keptPairs by pairing each PruneResult with its ScoredMessage before filtering drops. Use the paired scored entry (not array index) for category/score lookups. Also build scoreMap from keptPairs so modified messages map correctly.

name: batch2-unblocked-fixes
description: 3 unblocked issues via 2 leads in ~6 min. cc-fixes-lead handled sapling-48ad (tool name case normalization via toLowerCase) + closed sapling-a01f as already-fixed. prune-fix-lead handled sapling-a58d (preserved scored-result pairing through filter pipeline). Both clean merges.

content: branding-color-exports: color.ts exports brand (primary/accent/muted via chalk.rgb), icons (open/active/closed/blocked Set D), and printSuccess/printError/printWarning helpers. Brand palette: rgb(76,175,80) primary, rgb(255,183,77) accent, rgb(120,120,110) muted.

name: json-envelope-v2
description: JSON envelope uses { success: true, command: '<cmd>', ...data } / { success: false, command: '<cmd>', error: '<msg>', ...details }. printJson() and jsonOutput() now require command as first param. Old { name, version, ...data } / { error: { code, message } } format deprecated.

content: When --version and --json are both in process.argv, intercept before Commander parses and output JSON { name, version, runtime: 'bun', platform: '<platform>-<arch>' }. Check process.argv before program.parse().

commands-clean-builder pattern: new CLI commands go in src/commands/<name>.ts with register<Name>Command(program) export; index.ts wires them after existing commands; showSuggestionAfterError(false) set before registering typo handler to avoid duplicate suggestions

name: cli-polish-batch
description: 14 CLI polish issues across 3 parallel leads (branding 4, cli-core 5, commands 4+README) in ~25 min. 40 sessions total. File area partitioning: src/logging/ (branding), src/index.ts+json.ts+config.ts (cli-core), src/commands/ new dir (commands). All clean merges, test count 279→298.

estimateBlockTokens in measure.ts only handles text and tool_use blocks. When tool_result blocks (from loop.ts user messages) reach the context manager, it crashes with undefined.length. The bench harness works around this with its own estimateBenchBlockTokens. This makes Sapling completely unusable for any task requiring tools.
resolution: Add tool_result block handling to estimateBlockTokens: if block.type is tool_result, estimate from block.content string. Alternatively, match the bench harness approach with an unknown-block fallback.

description: BashTool awaits proc.exited before draining stdout/stderr pipes. This can deadlock if subprocess output exceeds the OS pipe buffer (~64KB). The CcClient already fixed this pattern using Promise.all([proc.exited, stdout.text(), stderr.text()]). GrepTool has the same issue.
resolution: Use Promise.all([proc.exited, stdout.text(), stderr.text()]) to drain concurrently, matching the CcClient pattern.

content: Sapling is completely non-functional for tool-using tasks in v0.1.1. Non-tool tasks (simple Q&A) work fine. The critical path is: loop.ts pushes ToolResultBlock[] as user message content → context manager → measureUtilization → estimateMessageTokens → estimateBlockTokens crashes on tool_result type. Fix sapling-2692 before any other work.

description: estimateBlockTokens in measure.ts crashed on tool_result blocks with 'undefined is not an object' because the else-branch assumed tool_use and accessed .name/.input.
resolution: Add explicit tool_result branch estimating from block.tool_use_id + block.content; update estimateMessageTokens cast; import ToolResultBlock.

description: quiet-flag-suppresses-answer: loop.ts used logger.info(finalText) for the final response, which got silenced by --quiet (logger sets level=error when quiet=true).
resolution: Use process.stdout.write() directly for the final answer text so --quiet only suppresses diagnostic log lines, not the primary output.

description: BashTool awaited proc.exited before draining stdout/stderr pipes, causing deadlock when output exceeded pipe buffer (~64KB).
resolution: Use Promise.all([proc.exited, stdout.text(), stderr.text()]) to drain concurrently. Matches CcClient pattern.

description: fish-completions-broken-condition: generateFish() built the no-subcommand condition as 'not A; or B; or C' (joining __fish_seen_subcommand_from calls with '; or '). In fish shell, 'not' only negates the immediately following command — B and C are always true, so the condition was always satisfied.
resolution: Use a single '__fish_seen_subcommand_from a b c ...' call with all subcommands as space-separated arguments; negate the whole thing with a single 'not'.

description: classifySdkError() only checks HTTP status codes; when SDK throws client-side auth error (missing ANTHROPIC_API_KEY) there is no HTTP status, so it returns SDK_API_ERROR (retriable) instead of SDK_AUTH_FAILED (unrecoverable), causing 3 retries.
resolution: Add AUTH_MESSAGE_RE fallback after status-based switch: /api.?key|anthropic_api_key|authentication|unauthorized/i — if message matches, return SDK_AUTH_FAILED.

name: cc-subprocess-timeout
description: CcClient timeout uses Promise.race([drainPromise, timeoutPromise]). Reject timeoutPromise BEFORE calling proc.kill() so the race resolves on the timeout path. Default 120_000ms. Throws ClientError CC_TIMEOUT. Test helper makeHangingProcess() returns proc.exited that never resolves.
biome-worktree-path-exclusion: Biome 2.4.5+ changed glob semantics — \!**/.overstory in files.includes matches the cwd itself when running biome check . from within an .overstory worktree path.
resolution: Use \!.overstory (no globstar, no trailing /**) which only excludes the dir at project root. Biome 2.2.0+ guidance: folder exclusions don't need /**.

description: bun run lint always exits code 1 in worktrees located inside .overstory/ because biome.json excludes '\!**/.overstory' — no .ts files are found.
resolution: Run lint from the canonical repo root, or accept that markdown-only tasks have no lint impact.

description: cli.ts readFile throws raw ENOENT when --system-prompt-file is missing
resolution: Wrap with try/catch; check (err as NodeJS.ErrnoException).code === 'ENOENT' and throw ConfigError('System prompt file not found: ...', 'CONFIG_FILE_NOT_FOUND').

content: SaplingError caught in index.ts run action: process.stderr.write('Error: '+err.message) + exitCode=1. Prevents raw stack traces from Commander. Catches all SaplingError subclasses (ConfigError, ClientError, ToolError, etc.).

name: v0.1.2-bugfix-batch
description: 14 issues across 5 parallel leads (runtime-fix 2, client-fix 2, cli-behavior 4, cli-tooling 3, docs 3) in ~17 min. All clean merges, no conflicts. 314 tests after all merges.

CC backend tool_result serialization: serializeContentBlock() in cc.ts only handles text and tool_use types. ToolResultBlock (type: tool_result) from loop.ts falls through to else branch producing '[Tool Call: undefined(undefined)]' because tool_result has no .name or .input. LLM never sees tool output, hallucinates completion.
resolution: Add tool_result branch to serializeContentBlock: if block.type === tool_result, serialize as [Tool Result for <tool_use_id>]: <content>. Also consider adding is_error indicator.

description: Config validation NaN bypass: validateConfig checks maxTurns < 1, but NaN < 1 is false in JS. --max-turns abc passes validation, loop exits immediately with max turns NaN reached after 0 turns.
resolution: Add Number.isNaN(merged.maxTurns) || !Number.isFinite(merged.maxTurns) check before the < 1 comparison in validateConfig.

description: CLI default values override environment variables: Commander option defaults for --model, --backend, --max-turns are always truthy, so they always appear in loadConfig overrides which spread after env vars. SAPLING_MODEL, SAPLING_BACKEND, SAPLING_MAX_TURNS env vars can never take effect.
resolution: Only include CLI values in overrides when explicitly provided by the user, not Commander defaults. Use Commander's parseOptions or check if option was explicitly set.

description: GrepTool pipe deadlock: awaiting proc.exited before draining stdout/stderr causes deadlock when rg output exceeds OS pipe buffer (~65KB).
resolution: Use Promise.all([proc.exited, stdout.text(), stderr.text()]) to drain concurrently, matching BashTool pattern (mx-f56cc1, mx-c64976).

description: CC backend tool_result serialization: serializeContentBlock() in cc.ts only handled text and tool_use types, producing '[Tool Call: undefined(undefined)]' for ToolResultBlock inputs.
resolution: Add tool_result branch to serializeContentBlock returning block.content; update serializeMessageContent to accept (ContentBlock | ToolResultBlock)[].

description: Config validation NaN bypass: validateConfig checks maxTurns < 1 and contextWindow < 1000, but NaN < 1 is false in JS.
resolution: Add Number.isNaN(val) || !Number.isFinite(val) check before the numeric comparison for both maxTurns and contextWindow.

description: cwd-ignored-in-tools: WriteTool, ReadTool, and EditTool named the cwd parameter _cwd and ignored it. When LLM provides relative paths, files were written/read relative to process.cwd() instead of --cwd.
resolution: Rename _cwd to cwd and resolve file_path at start of execute(): const resolved = isAbsolute(filePath) ? filePath : resolve(cwd, filePath); use resolved for all subsequent file ops.

name: json-output-separation
description: loop.ts must not write directly to stdout. Final response text should be returned in LoopResult.responseText; index.ts emits it as printJson('response',{response}) in --json mode or raw stdout in normal mode. This ensures clean NDJSON when --json is active.

content: --timing output in index.ts must check process.stderr.isTTY before applying colors.dim(). Use: process.stderr.write(process.stderr.isTTY ? colors.dim(msg) : msg). The setColorEnabled() call only affects the logger, not direct color calls.

description: CcClient pushed structured.thinking as a text ContentBlock; loop.ts then concatenated ALL text blocks including thinking and printed them to stdout, leaking internal LLM reasoning into user output.
resolution: Log thinking at debug level only; never add to content array. Update tests to assert thinking block is absent from response content.

content: cli-commander-defaults: Remove Commander .option() 3rd arg (default value) for --model, --backend, --max-turns so env vars (SAPLING_MODEL, SAPLING_BACKEND, SAPLING_MAX_TURNS) are not overridden when flags are absent. DEFAULT_CONFIG in config.ts provides defaults instead.
cli-stdin-support: sp run supports piped stdin via Bun.stdin.text() when prompt arg is absent and \!process.stdin.isTTY. Command changed from 'run <prompt>' to 'run [prompt]' making prompt optional.

name: v0.1.3-fixes-batch
description: 13 issues across 4 parallel leads (cc-client 3, tools 3, cli-config 5, output 2) in ~13 min. sapling-4055 (subprocess-per-turn) closed as wont_fix — session persistence incompatible with context pipeline by design. 12 issues fixed, all clean-merge except 1 auto-resolve.

title: CC backend: no session persistence
rationale: Sapling's context pipeline (measure→score→prune→archive→reshape) modifies the message array each turn. Session persistence via --resume would create duplicate/conflicting context since the claude process maintains its own unmanaged history. SDK backend (Anthropic API) is the production path; CC backend is a convenience fallback that re-serializes per turn by design.

name: pi-backend-wiring
description: Pi backend wired as LlmBackend union member. PiClient mirrors CcClient structure: subprocess spawn, --model flag accepts provider/model format (e.g. google/gemini-2.0-flash), JSON output parsing. PI_NOT_FOUND added to UNRECOVERABLE_CODES in loop.ts. VALID_BACKENDS in config.ts must be kept in sync with LlmBackend union in types.ts.

name: pi-client-design
description: PiClient spawns 'pi -p --mode json --no-tools -e <ext> --system-prompt ... --model ... conversation' per turn. Extension file (generated per-call, cleaned up in finally{}) registers tool schemas via registerTool()/Type.Unsafe(). JSONL stdout parsed for message_end event; mapContent() normalizes tool names to lowercase; mapStopReason() maps toolUse->tool_use, maxTokens->max_tokens. Error codes: PI_TIMEOUT, PI_FAILED, PI_NO_RESPONSE, PI_INVALID_OUTPUT, PI_NOT_FOUND (ENOENT from spawn). Uses IIFE to keep proc const-typed for Bun pipe overload inference.

name: pi-test-patterns
description: PiClient tests follow cc.test.ts patterns exactly: makeFakeProcess/makeHangingProcess helpers, spyOn(Bun, 'spawn'), makeMessageEndOutput()/makeAgentEndOutput() JSONL helpers. TypeScript note: stop-reason union literal type needed in test case arrays to satisfy expect().toBe() type check.

name: pi-backend-batch
description: 3 issues (wiring, core impl, tests) for Pi multi-provider backend via 1 lead in ~21 min. Sequential dependency chain: becf→f9bd→797f. Lead spawned 3 builders sequentially. Auto-resolve on pi.ts add/add conflict (stub→full impl). Final: 354 tests, 27 new.

CC backend tool calling is non-functional: --tools '' disables tool calling entirely, and injecting tool definitions into the system prompt text doesn't cause the model to produce structured tool_calls in --json-schema output. The model responds with text_response instead.
resolution: Needs architectural redesign. Options: use CC's allowedTools + custom MCP, use stream-json to intercept, or fall back to SDK backend for now.

description: Pi backend tool calling is non-functional: --no-tools + extension with no-op handlers causes Pi to run its own agent loop, model hallucinates tool responses in text, returns stopReason 'stop' with no tool_use blocks. Token usage also reports 0/0 because Pi uses 'input'/'output' fields not 'input_tokens'/'output_tokens'.
resolution: Needs Pi backend redesign. Either register extensions with real handlers, parse JSONL stream for tool_use interception, or accept Pi as an agent and capture output.

title: SDK backend is the only functional path for tool calling
rationale: CC and Pi backends inject tool defs as text or no-op extensions, but neither produces native tool_use content blocks. The SDK backend uses Anthropic's messages.create() with tools parameter, which returns proper tool_use blocks that Sapling's loop can dispatch. This is the only backend that can work for the MVP without architectural changes to CC/Pi.

description: Commit 9e364a0 (stdin support + empty prompt validation) wholesale-replaced the run action handler in index.ts, silently dropping both the responseText output fix (abcc87b) and the TTY-safe timing fix (sapling-e9aa). Two previously-closed issues regressed without any test catching it.
resolution: Need end-to-end tests that verify responseText appears in stdout and ANSI codes are suppressed on non-TTY stderr. Agent tasks that rewrite large code blocks should diff against HEAD to avoid dropping unrelated fixes.

name: fix-then-regress
description: Multiple agents working on Sapling in parallel via Overstory. Each agent fixes a narrow issue, adds unit tests, and closes the issue. But a subsequent agent rewrites the same file and drops the fix. The unit test mocks prevent detection because they test parsing logic, not end-to-end behavior. 5 previously-closed issues (sapling-501a, sapling-4e5d, sapling-48ad, sapling-49eb, sapling-5b1a) were all 'fixed' but the agent remained non-functional in v0.1.1 and v0.1.2.

name: integration-test-pattern
description: Integration tests in src/integration.test.ts are gated behind SAPLING_INTEGRATION_TESTS=1 env var using describe.skipIf(SKIP). Use SDK backend with claude-haiku-4-5-20251001 for minimal cost. 4 tests: read file, create file, bash output, CLI stdout. responseText must be printed in index.ts run action to pass stdout test.

content: loop.ts returns responseText in LoopResult; index.ts run action must write it to process.stdout (process.stdout.write(result.responseText+newline)) before the JSON block so the agent final response appears in CLI output. Missing this causes test 4 failures.

name: phase1-phase2-batch
description: 5 issues (3 regressions + integration tests + SDK validation) across 2 parallel leads in ~10 min. Phase 1 lead (phase1-regressions) fixed responseText output and TTY-safe timing in src/index.ts. Phase 2 lead (integration-tests) created src/integration.test.ts with 4 integration tests gated behind SAPLING_INTEGRATION_TESTS=1 using SDK backend + haiku model.
Phase 2 lead builder independently added responseText stdout printing to src/index.ts, duplicating Phase 1 fix. Merged cleanly but was unnecessary work.
resolution: Send file-scope warnings BEFORE lead spawns builder. When dispatching leads with potentially overlapping files, explicitly exclude files owned by other leads in the dispatch mail.

name: guards-file-wiring
description: --guards-file <path> option wired through RunOptions.guardsFile → SaplingConfig.guardsFile → loadGuardConfig() in config.ts. Returns null (standalone mode) when file absent, throws ConfigError for invalid JSON or missing 'rules' array. HookManager stub in src/hooks/manager.ts accepts GuardConfig; LoopOptions.hookManager: unknown for forward-compat with sapling-8350.

name: smoke-test-strategy
description: CC backend smoke tests in integration.test.ts use claudeAvailable=false guard (beforeAll checks 'claude --version') so tests skip gracefully when claude CLI absent. All 3 tests gated behind SAPLING_INTEGRATION_TESTS=1 with same SKIP flag as SDK tests. Test 2 (tool_calls assertion) is expected to FAIL due to cc-plain-text-fallback — intentional to expose the regression.

name: hook-system-design
description: HookManager evaluates GuardConfig rules in order for pre/post tool call events. Rule matching: rule.tool===undefined matches all tools; specific tool name matches only that tool. Actions pre-call: block→return false (tool skipped, error result returned to LLM), warn→log+continue, allow→return true immediately (short-circuit). Actions post-call: warn only (block/allow are no-ops). IHookManager interface defined in types.ts to avoid circular import with hooks/manager.ts. hookManager passed through LoopOptions into loop.ts tool dispatch.

name: guards-evaluator-design
description: guards.ts exports pure sync evaluator functions (checkPathBoundary, checkFileScope, checkReadOnly, checkBlockedBashPatterns, checkBlockedTools, evaluateGuards). GuardConfig extended with flat fields (pathBoundary/fileScope/readOnly/blockedBashPatterns/blockedTools). HookManager runs evaluateGuards() before rules array in preToolCall(). All guards use path.resolve() for normalization — no filesystem access.

name: events-emitter-design
description: EventEmitter class in src/hooks/events.ts: LoopOptions.eventEmitter uses minimal inline type { emit(event: Record<string, unknown>): void } to avoid circular imports. emit() auto-injects ISO timestamp. Convenience methods (started/turnStart/toolCall/toolResult/turnEnd/runComplete) call emit() internally. contextUtilization in turn_end is a 0.0-1.0 ratio (total.used/total.budget). Events only emitted when config.json=true, wired in cli.ts.

name: events-guardconfig-merge-conflict
description: When events-builder added eventEmitter to LoopOptions in types.ts, it branched from a pre-guards version and stripped the GuardConfig flat fields. Fix-builder pattern: reconcile by adding eventEmitter back to the canonical branch types.ts (which already has GuardConfig fields), producing a clean merge target with all fields present.

name: integration-features-batch
description: 5 issues across 3 dependency waves (wave1: guards-file CLI + smoke tests parallel, wave2: hook system, wave3: guard rules + NDJSON events parallel) via 5 leads in ~40 min. 7 builders total (1 fix builder for events types.ts regression). Clean merges throughout.
events-builder removed GuardConfig flat fields from types.ts when adding EventEmitter types. Lead caught it and spawned events-fix-builder.
resolution: When dispatching builders that touch shared type files like types.ts, instruct them to only add/modify their own types and preserve existing fields. Leads should verify type completeness in review.

name: rpc-stdin-listener
description: RPC mode stdin reading must use Bun.stdin.stream() with manual line buffering, not Bun.stdin.text() which reads entire input upfront. RPC server runs in src/hooks/rpc.ts, queues commands (steer/followUp/abort), processes between loop turns (after tool results appended, before context manager runs).

title: RPC message injection strategy
rationale: Steered content from RPC steer command should be appended to current turn's tool results (not new user turn). Simpler, less disruptive, keeps turn atomic. Injection point: src/loop.ts line 335 after toolResultBlocks pushed.

name: rpc-feature-spec
description: Comprehensive RPC protocol design and implementation roadmap at .overstory/specs/sapling-232e.md. Covers protocol format, file structure, unknowns, and recommended next steps for builder agent. Key files: types.ts, config.ts, index.ts, cli.ts, loop.ts, hooks/rpc.ts (new).

content: RPC stdin channel must use Bun.stdin.stream() with manual line buffering (not Bun.stdin.text()). Pattern: read stream → decode chunk → scan buffer for newlines → parseLine(). Implemented in src/rpc/channel.ts as RpcChannel class with async startReading() and drained:Promise<void> for test synchronization.

name: rpc-module-structure
description: RPC feature uses src/rpc/ directory: types.ts (RpcRequest union), channel.ts (RpcChannel FIFO queue), server.ts (RpcServer wraps channel+EventEmitter, emits rpc_request_ack), index.ts (re-exports). IRpcServer minimal interface in src/types.ts avoids circular deps. AbortRequest sets flag, never queued.

content: rpc-mode-stdin-conflict: In --mode rpc, stdin is the control channel so prompt MUST come from CLI args. --json is implicitly enabled when rpcMode=true. Guard added in src/index.ts before stdin read; error message includes hint about RPC mode.

name: rpc-mode-design
description: rpc-mode-design: --mode rpc flag enables JSON-RPC stdin control channel. RpcChannel reads stdin line-by-line with manual buffering. Methods: steer (inject user message into tool results), followUp (queue next prompt), abort (flag-based loop exit). Loop checks abort before each turn. --json implicitly enabled in RPC mode. New module: src/rpc/ (types, channel, server + tests). 756 lines, 27 new RPC tests + 4 loop integration tests.

name: rpc-batch-efficiency
description: Single lead with 2 scouts + 1 builder delivered full RPC feature (sapling-232e) in ~22 min. Lead self-verified (no separate reviewer needed). 470 tests passing post-merge. Clean merge, no conflicts.

content: ANTHROPIC_AUTH_TOKEN is supported as a fallback for ANTHROPIC_API_KEY in loadConfig(). Resolution: process.env.ANTHROPIC_API_KEY ?? process.env.ANTHROPIC_AUTH_TOKEN. Stored as apiKey in SaplingConfig and passed to AnthropicClient.

name: model-alias-resolution
description: resolveModelAlias(model) in config.ts checks ANTHROPIC_DEFAULT_{UPPERCASE_ALIAS}_MODEL env var (e.g. ANTHROPIC_DEFAULT_SONNET_MODEL for 'sonnet'). Called in validateConfig() so all entry paths benefit. Full model strings pass through unchanged.

name: eventConfig-lifecycle-hooks
description: EventConfig in GuardConfig (guards.json) has onToolStart/onToolEnd (fire-and-forget Bun.spawn) and onSessionEnd (awaited Bun.spawn). Tool heartbeats prevent overstory watchdog from marking agent stale; onSessionEnd must be awaited for session bookkeeping (metrics, state transition, worker_done mail).

name: auth-store-fallback
description: loadConfig() is async — reads ~/.sapling/auth.json (via readAuthStore()) as fallback when ANTHROPIC_API_KEY env var is not set. resolveProvider(model) maps model name to provider key (MiniMax* → minimax, default → anthropic). PROVIDER_BASE_URLS holds known defaults (minimax → https://api.minimax.io/anthropic). Auth store creds provide both apiKey and baseUrl to AnthropicClient.

content: loadConfig() in src/config.ts is async (returns Promise<SaplingConfig>). All callers must await it.

event-stream fixup pattern: cherry-pick in-scope commits from a diverged worker branch rather than reverting individual files; cherry-pick naturally brings only the targeted commit's file changes, leaving out-of-scope files untouched

loop-followUp-injection: followUp RPC requests are injected as a new standalone user message ({ role: 'user', content: string }) after tool results, while steer appends a [STEER] text block to the existing tool results user message. Injection point is Step 7b in loop.ts, after all tool calls complete.

name: loop-events-drain
description: RPC dequeue in loop.ts uses while loop to drain all queued requests per turn. steer appends text block to current tool results user message; followUp pushes standalone user message. Both use [METHOD] prefix for LLM visibility.

content: EventEmitter type discriminators in loop.ts: ready (was started), tool_start (was tool_call), tool_end (was tool_result), result (was run_complete). turn_start and turn_end unchanged.

name: rpc-getstate-design
description: getState JSON-RPC method adds {jsonrpc:'2.0',id:N,method:'getState'} request that responds synchronously with {jsonrpc:'2.0',id:N,result:{status:'idle'|'working'|'error',currentTool?:string}}. RpcServer tracks state via setWorking(toolName)/setIdle()/setError() public methods. getState is NOT queued in RpcChannel (handled immediately via onParsed callback). Writer function is injected for testability (defaults to process.stdout.write). No changes to types.ts or loop.ts required.

content: followUp RPC injection uses bare content (no prefix). steer uses [STEER] prefix. argsSummary in tool_start event must be computed inline: JSON.stringify(call.input).slice(0, 200)

name: progress-event-design
description: EventEmitter.progress(percent, subtask, filesChanged) emits {type:'progress', percent:N, subtask:'description', filesChanged:N} NDJSON events. percent=0..100 estimated from turn/maxTurns ratio. subtask is human-readable activity label (e.g. 'Running tests'). Consumed by overstory dashboard for real-time per-agent visibility.

name: rpc-events-batch-merge-order
description: When merging branches that touch overlapping files (e.g., multiple branches modifying src/loop.ts), merge the most foundational changes first (event type renames before method wiring). Auto-resolve tier may silently produce artifacts like duplicate prefixes — always run tests after auto-resolve merges.

content: ctx-v1-types: src/context/v1/types.ts defines all v1 pipeline data models: Turn, TurnMetadata, Operation (OperationStatus/OperationType), PipelineInput/Output/State, BudgetUtilization, BoundarySignals, EvalWeights, TurnHint, and constants BOUNDARY_WEIGHTS (0.35/0.30/0.20/0.15), BOUNDARY_THRESHOLD (0.5), EVAL_WEIGHTS (recency 0.25, fileOverlap 0.25, causalDependency 0.25, outcomeSignificance 0.15, operationType 0.10), RECENCY_HALF_LIFE_OPS (4), V1_BUDGET_ALLOCATIONS (systemWithArchive 0.25, activeOperations 0.25, headroom 0.50), TOOL_OUTPUT_TRUNCATION thresholds.

name: evaluate-stage-design
description: evaluate.ts exports individual scoring functions (recencyScore, fileOverlapScore, causalDependencyScore, outcomeSignificanceScore, operationTypeScore, evaluateOperation) and a stage entry point evaluate(operations) that updates op.score in-place. Uses EVAL_WEIGHTS and RECENCY_HALF_LIFE_OPS constants from types.ts. opsAgo = totalOps-1-op.id when activeOp exists, else 0. Decision content bonus (+0.2) applied inside outcomeSignificanceScore using turns[].meta.hasDecision.

name: ingest-stage-design
description: src/context/v1/ingest.ts implements turn extraction (extractTurns pairs assistant+toolResults into Turn objects), boundary detection (detectBoundary uses 4 weighted signals: toolTypeTransition 0.35, fileScopeChange 0.30, intentSignal 0.20, temporalGap 0.15, threshold 0.5), and operation registry management. User message ToolResultBlock[] cast via 'as unknown as ContentBlock[]' to satisfy Message type. All constants imported from types.ts.

name: compact-stage-design
description: compact.ts exports truncateToolOutput(toolName, content), compactOperation(op), truncateOperationOutputs(op), compact(operations, activeOperationId). Threshold: COMPACTION_SCORE_THRESHOLD=0.3 (from types.ts). Active op is never compacted. Already-compacted/archived ops are skipped. Kept ops get tool output truncated in-place on turn.toolResults content blocks (ToolResultBlock[]).

name: templates-module-design
description: templates.ts exports extractPurpose(op) [regex cascade on first turn's assistant text, fallback to type+files], buildActionSummary(op) [deduplicated tool(file) list], describeOutcome(op), renderCompactSummary(op) [multi-line: [Op N] purpose, Actions:, Outcome:], renderArchiveEntry(op) [one-liner: Op{id}: purpose [outcome]].

name: budget-stage-design
description: budget.ts exports operationTokens(op), enforceBudget(ops, systemTokens, window), enforceArchiveBudget(entries, personaTokens, window), and budget() stage entry point. enforceArchiveBudget uses FIFO drop (not greedy skip) — drops oldest entries in sequence until total fits, not skipping non-fitting entries. enforceBudget sorts completed/compacted by score desc and greedily fills the 25% operation budget; active op always retained unconditionally.
